title: Claude Code marketplace plugin submission
description: Register token-pilot as a first-class Claude Code plugin so users can install with one command (/plugin install token-pilot) instead of the npx + install-agents two-step. Bundle already exists in .claude-plugin/: plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json (Read + Edit + SessionStart), skills/ via ../skills reference, and dist/agents/*.md shipped in the npm tarball. Required steps: finalise plugin.json metadata, submit to anthropics/claude-plugins-official or publish our own marketplace.json, verify auto-load works for agents + skills + hooks without needing token-pilot init. This also finally makes skills reachable for the majority of users.

title: Tier 2 tp-* subagent pack (5 agents)
description: Ship five Tier 2 agents predicted in TP-816 section 5.4.2, following the Tier 1 pattern (shared preamble + role block + response contract, frontmatter with mcp__token-pilot__* tools, response budget): tp-dead-code-hunter (find_unused + find_usages; budget 400), tp-api-surveyor (module_info + outline + find_usages; 500), tp-history-explorer (smart_log + smart_diff; 500), tp-audit-scanner (code_audit + find_usages; 500), tp-session-restorer (session_snapshot; 300). Each goes through prompt-contract tests + fixture-agent compat like Tier 1. Rolling out agent-by-agent is fine вҖ” telemetry-gated per TP-816 section 5.4.7.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bash output truncator (PostToolUse hook for Bash tool)
description: New PostToolUse hook on the Bash tool, separate from the Read hook. When Bash output exceeds a configurable size (default ~200 lines or ~8000 chars), replace body with head 40 + tail 20 lines + note + path to full output in .token-pilot/bash-output/<ts>.log. Covers npm test, git log --no-pager, find, grep with many matches. Respects TOKEN_PILOT_BYPASS. Opt-out via config hooks.truncateBashOutput=false. Expected savings: very high on test-heavy and exploration sessions (tests alone can dump 10k+ lines of irrelevant passing-test noise).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.21.0. TP-c08: doctor advisor surfaces CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL/MAX_THINKING_TOKENS/CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE/model=sonnet tips. TP-rtg: .claudeignore generator (managed-marker, non-destructive) + CLAUDE.md hygiene check in doctor. TP-jzh: re-framed as Bash output advisor after Context7 confirmed PostToolUse cannot truncate tool_response for non-MCP Bash tool вҖ” now a PostToolUse:Bash hook that appends a one-line tip when output exceeds ~8000 chars. 843 tests, tsc clean. Release https://github.com/Digital-Threads/token-pilot/releases/tag/v0.21.0

title: .claudeignore generator + CLAUDE.md hygiene doctor check
description: Two one-time wins that compound every message. (a) On token-pilot init, offer to create .claudeignore with sensible defaults (node_modules, dist, lockfiles, caches, generated code). Ecosystem-standard file, works for other tools too. Merge with our config.ignore. (b) token-pilot doctor warns when project CLAUDE.md exceeds 60 non-empty lines вҖ” that file loads into EVERY Claude Code message, so a 200-line CLAUDE.md costs thousands of tokens per turn. Follow-up (separate PR): interactive split-assistant that suggests docs/ fragments to load on demand.

title: Session-scoped dedup: seen-in-session marker + diff-since-last-read + grouped find_usages + Write-invalidation
description: Extract more savings from what the hook already intercepts. Four tightly-related mechanics: (a) when the same file+session+content-hash was summarised before, return a 1-line pointer 'already seen this session; use read_symbol for specifics', not a new summary; (b) if hash differs since last time, return only read_diff since prior; (c) find_usages groups identical signatures across N files into one line instead of N blocks; (d) PostToolUse on Edit/Write invalidates our file cache + project_overview cache so next read is fresh. Must survive /clear (new session_id вҶ’ clean slate). Benchmark target: additional 20-30 percent saving on the hook path.
close_reason: Closed

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] hook-composition test: mock plugin returns permissionDecision:'allow' + its own additionalContext; our deny-enhanced hook fires; final tool result is denied; both additionalContext strings accumulated into the denial message (if Claude Code behaves as documented) OR at minimum our deny wins (В§12 fallback)
- [ ] Golden comparison test: same onboarding task run under tp-run vs general-purpose fixture harness вҶ’ tp-run tokens вүӨ 40% of general-purpose tokens (regression if slips)

## Subtasks

- [ ] **7.1** Write hook-composition E2E test using a small shell harness that simulates Claude Code's hook chain.
- [ ] **7.2** Write tp-run vs general-purpose golden-task harness (can simulate by running both agents' system prompts against the same scripted task via the agent-builder output + direct LLM call OR via a cheaper stub that counts tool calls вҖ” choose pragmatic approach).

# Phase 6 вҖ” Telemetry, config migration, stats

## Goal
Production-quality telemetry and a config-migration pass so v0.19 users upgrade cleanly.

## Files
- Modify: src/core/hook-tracker.ts (or wherever hook-denied.jsonl is currently written вҖ” centralise to .token-pilot/hook-events.jsonl with new schema)
- Create: src/core/event-log.ts (rotation + retention)
- Modify: skills/stats/SKILL.md + src/handlers/ (command for --session and --by-agent views)
- Modify: src/config/loader.ts (auto-migrate legacy mode:"deny" вҶ’ "advisory"; emit one-time stderr migration notice; idempotent so second run is silent)
- Modify: src/types.ts (finalise config shape)
- Create: tests/core/event-log.test.ts
- Create: tests/config/migration.test.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hook-events JSONL schema: {ts, session_id, agent_type, agent_id, event, file, lines, estTokens, summaryTokens, savedTokens}
- [ ] Rotation: when current file > 10 MB вҶ’ rename to hook-events.<unix-ts>.jsonl; new file starts empty
- [ ] Retention: files older than 30 days deleted on startup; total directory size cap 100 MB enforced (oldest deleted first if exceeded)
- [ ] `token-pilot stats` (current) shows totals + average savings
- [ ] `token-pilot stats --session` filters to current session_id only (read from env if MCP context provides it, else most recent)
- [ ] `token-pilot stats --by-agent` groups by agent_type, sorted by savedTokens desc
- [ ] Config migration: on startup if config has mode:"deny" вҶ’ rewrite to mode:"advisory" + emit stderr notice once per file (track migration in config as 'migratedFrom: deny' to avoid double-notice)
- [ ] env TOKEN_PILOT_DENY_THRESHOLD overrides config.hooks.denyThreshold; documented
- [ ] Tests cover: schema round-trip; rotation at threshold; retention removes old files only; migration idempotent

## Subtasks

- [ ] **6.1** Centralise hook-event writing: all hook exit paths that currently write to .token-pilot/hook-denied.jsonl now write to hook-events.jsonl with new schema.
- [ ] **6.2** Implement event-log.ts: append, rotate-if-needed, retention-on-startup. Tests with fake fs time.
- [ ] **6.3** Extend stats handler with --session and --by-agent flags.
- [ ] **6.4** Implement config migration for legacy deny mode. Idempotent. Tests with before/after configs.
- [ ] **6.5** Document new fields in skills/stats/SKILL.md and README config section.
- [ ] **6.6** Lint + tests green.

## Done-check
All tests green + manual smoke ('token-pilot stats --by-agent' on a session with real events) вҶ’ `bd close <this-id>`. Phase 7 unblocks.

design:
# Phase 5 вҖ” install-agents CLI

## Goal
Ship Tier 1 agents to user's filesystem via explicit CLI command. Scope always chosen by user (user vs project). No silent writes.

## Files
- Modify: package.json (add "files": dist/agents/**/*.md; prepublishOnly hook renders templates вҶ’ dist/agents)
- Modify: tsconfig.json or build script (ensure dist/agents copy step)
- Create: scripts/build-agents.mjs (renders templates/agents/<name>.md via agent-builder.ts вҶ’ dist/agents/<name>.md)
- Create: src/cli/install-agents.ts (interactive + non-interactive install)
- Create: src/cli/uninstall-agents.ts
- Modify: src/index.ts (add 'install-agents' and 'uninstall-agents' cases)
- Modify: src/index.ts startServer or wherever MCP starts вҖ” add 'agents absent' stderr reminder unless suppressed
- Modify: src/config/loader.ts (add agents.scope, agents.reminder fields)
- Modify: src/types.ts (config shape)
- Create: tests/cli/install-agents.test.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `npm run build` produces dist/agents/tp-*.md (all 6)
- [ ] `npx token-pilot install-agents` without flags (TTY): prompts 'user/project?' and writes to chosen location
- [ ] `--scope=user` writes to ~/.claude/agents/tp-*.md
- [ ] `--scope=project` writes to ./.claude/agents/tp-*.md
- [ ] Non-TTY without --scope вҶ’ error 'must specify --scope in non-interactive mode'
- [ ] After write: stderr prints 'Installed N agents to <scope>. Start a new Claude Code session to see them.'
- [ ] Idempotence: each written file has frontmatter token_pilot_version + token_pilot_content_hash. Re-running install: if content_hash matches template вҶ’ update in place silently; if differs but file marked user-edited (hash missing or stale + body modified) вҶ’ print 'skipping tp-X: edited by user, use --force to override'
- [ ] `--force` overrides idempotence
- [ ] `uninstall-agents [--scope=...]` removes only files whose frontmatter shows token_pilot_content_hash present (never touches user files). Scope required (no global default).
- [ ] agents.scope config field is persisted after first install (so re-runs and upgrades use the same scope without asking)
- [ ] At MCP server startup: if no tp-* found in any scope AND agents.reminder != false AND TOKEN_PILOT_NO_AGENT_REMINDER not set вҶ’ emit ONE stderr line: '[token-pilot] tp-* agents not installed. Run `npx token-pilot install-agents` to enable guaranteed-savings subagents (scope: user or project вҖ” your choice).'
- [ ] Reminder is NEVER repeated within same process lifetime
- [ ] Reminder does not fire if MCP started inside a subagent (detectable via absence of interactive tty? acceptable proxy: skip if env var TOKEN_PILOT_SUBAGENT=1)

## Subtasks

- [ ] **5.1** Write scripts/build-agents.mjs: iterate templates/agents/*.md; for each, run agent-builder to get final content; write to dist/agents/<name>.md. Wire into package.json 'build' or 'prebuild'.
- [ ] **5.2** Update package.json 'files' array to include dist/agents. Verify `npm pack` contains the agents.
- [ ] **5.3** Implement install-agents CLI: scope resolution (flag > config > prompt); copy dist/agents/* to target; write frontmatter hash+version.
- [ ] **5.4** Implement idempotence logic: on re-run, compare frontmatter hash vs current template hash; update unchanged; warn on user-edited; --force override.
- [ ] **5.5** Implement uninstall-agents CLI with required --scope.
- [ ] **5.6** Implement MCP startup reminder: on startServer, check target scopes for any tp-*.md with valid frontmatter; if none and not suppressed, emit one stderr line.
- [ ] **5.7** Add agents.* config fields and env override TOKEN_PILOT_NO_AGENT_REMINDER.
- [ ] **5.8** Tests per acceptance criteria вҖ” use tmp dirs as HOME and CWD in tests to avoid polluting real env.
- [ ] **5.9** Manual smoke: in a scratch project, run install-agents --scope=project, confirm 6 files in .claude/agents/, verify one has correct frontmatter.
- [ ] **5.10** Lint + tests green.

## Done-check
All acceptance criteria green вҶ’ `bd close <this-id>`. Phase 6 unblocks.
close_reason: Phase 5 done: build-agents.mjs + dist/agents bundling, install-agents CLI (core + wrapper, idempotence matrix, --force, persisted agents.scope), uninstall-agents (required --scope), MCP startup reminder with single-fire + env/config suppression. 774 tests green, tsc clean. # Phase 3 вҖ” bless-agents CLI

## Goal
Let users upgrade third-party Category-C subagents to use token-pilot MCP with one command, non-destructively.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `bd bless-agents` interactive: lists classified agents (A/B skipped, C as candidates); prompts [a]ll / [i]nteractive / [n]o

# Phase 2 вҖ” SessionStart Reminder Hook

## Goal
Register a SessionStart hook that emits one additionalContext block containing the MCP tool rules and the auto-listed tp-* agents present in the project.

## Files

- Modify: src/hooks/installer.ts (install SessionStart alongside existing PreToolUse; keep idempotent)
- Create: src/hooks/session-start.ts (handler вҖ” scans agent directories, builds message, returns JSON)
- Modify: src/index.ts (add 'hook-session-start' case to main switch)
- Modify: src/types.ts + src/config/loader.ts (add sessionStart.{enabled, showStats, maxReminderTokens})
- Create: tests/hooks/session-start.test.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] hook-session-start CLI entrypoint reads stdin (ignores, SessionStart has no input payload) and emits JSON with additionalContext
- [ ] Message body is the MANDATORY + WHEN DELEGATING template from TP-816 В§5.2, with <auto-list> expanded from actual files found in ~/.claude/agents/tp-*.md and ./.claude/agents/tp-*.md
- [ ] If no tp-* agents found вҶ’ the WHEN DELEGATING section is still present but lists 'none installed вҖ” run npx token-pilot install-agents'

- [ ] Test: no agents installed вҶ’ message says 'run install-agents'
- [ ] Test: 3 tp-* agents present вҶ’ message lists those 3 with their descriptions

## Subtasks

# Phase 1 вҖ” Enhanced Deny Hook

## Goal
Replace current advisory-style deny in handleHookRead with a structural summary delivered inside permissionDecisionReason.

## Files
- Modify: src/index.ts (handleHookRead, lines ~165-237)
- Create: src/hooks/summary-pipeline.ts (orchestrates ast-index вҶ’ regex вҶ’ head-tail fallback chain)
- Create: src/hooks/summary-regex.ts (in-process regex parser)
- Create: src/hooks/summary-head-tail.ts (trivial last-resort)
- Create: src/hooks/summary-ast-index.ts (invokes bundled ast-index binary as subprocess)
- Create: tests/hooks/summary-pipeline.test.ts
- Create: tests/hooks/handleHookRead.test.ts
- Modify: src/types.ts (add 'deny-enhanced' to mode union)
- Modify: src/config/loader.ts (accept deny-enhanced mode; keep advisory; drop deprecated deny with auto-migration to advisory)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Config mode union is {'off','advisory','deny-enhanced'} вҖ” old 'deny' вҶ’ auto-migrated to 'advisory' with stderr notice
- [ ] handleHookRead in deny-enhanced mode returns hookSpecificOutput with permissionDecision:deny and permissionDecisionReason containing: header line + Imports section + Exports/Public symbols section + Declarations section + footer hint (bounded Read / read_for_edit / env bypass)
- [ ] Summary pipeline: tries ast-index subprocess first; falls to regex; falls to head+tail; falls to pass-through (allow) on any catastrophic error
- [ ] Summary total output respects summaryMaxTokens cap (default 1200)
- [ ] Non-code extensions pass through (as now)
- [ ] Bounded Reads (offset or limit present) pass through (as now)
- [ ] All paths tested: large code file вҶ’ deny-enhanced; small code file вҶ’ pass; config file вҶ’ pass; bounded Read вҶ’ pass; non-existent path вҶ’ pass; ast-index missing вҶ’ regex fallback; regex fails вҶ’ head-tail; head-tail fails вҶ’ pass-through
- [ ] Path-safety check (realpath + startsWith(projectRoot)) on the file argument before reading

## Subtasks (linear order, TDD for each)

- [ ] **1.1** Add 'deny-enhanced' to mode type union in src/types.ts. Drop old 'deny' literal.
- [ ] **1.2** Update config loader: auto-migrate `mode: "deny"` вҶ’ `"advisory"` with a stderr notice; reject unknown modes with clear error. Tests for both migration paths.
- [ ] **1.3** Write regex summary parser (summary-regex.ts): extracts imports, exports, class/function/interface/type declarations per language. Tests: TS, JS, Python, Go fixtures. Token-count the output.
- [ ] **1.4** Write head+tail fallback (summary-head-tail.ts): first 40 + last 20 lines with header 'parse failed'. Test with empty file, single-line file, binary-with-code-ext.
- [ ] **1.5** Write ast-index subprocess bridge (summary-ast-index.ts): findBinary вҶ’ spawn `ast-index outline <path>` вҶ’ parse output вҶ’ map to unified Summary interface. Test with ast-index present and missing.
- [ ] **1.6** Write pipeline orchestrator (summary-pipeline.ts): try ast-index вҶ’ regex вҶ’ head-tail вҶ’ pass-through signal. Returns {ok: Summary} | {passThrough: true, reason: string}. Tests for each fallback triggering.
- [ ] **1.7** Modify handleHookRead in src/index.ts: in deny-enhanced mode, run summary-pipeline; if result is passThrough вҶ’ exit 0 no output; otherwise format permissionDecisionReason and return the hookSpecificOutput JSON. Preserve existing behaviours for advisory and off modes.
- [ ] **1.8** Add path-safety check: realpathSync(filePath) must startsWith(projectRoot + path.sep). If not вҶ’ pass-through + stderr log. Test with ../../etc/passwd style inputs.
- [ ] **1.9** Integration test: scripted stdin + assert stdout JSON structure for all 5 scenarios (large code, small code, config, bounded, non-existent).
- [ ] **1.10** Verify lint + all tests green: `npm run lint && npm test`.
- [ ] **1.11** Commit (single commit per subtask is fine; group if needed). Suggested final commit: 'feat(hook): deny-enhanced mode with summary pipeline'.

## Verification commands
- Lint: `npm run lint`
- Unit tests: `npm test -- tests/hooks/`
- Manual smoke: `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"src/index.ts"}}' | node dist/index.js hook-read` вҶ’ expect JSON with permissionDecision:deny and a structural summary visible in permissionDecisionReason.

## Done-check
When all checkboxes above are ticked AND `npm test` is green AND `git status` is clean: `bd close <this-id>`. Phase 2 will auto-unblock.

design:
# Implementation Plan вҖ” token-pilot v0.20

**Design reference:** TP-816 (full spec in --design of that issue; operational checklist in --notes).

## Resume procedure (for any new session)

1. `bd prime` to load beads context.
2. `bd ready` вҶ’ see the next unblocked phase.
3. `bd show <phase-id>` вҶ’ read the --design to see checkboxed subtasks + acceptance criteria.
4. Work subtasks in order. Use `bd update <phase-id> --notes "progress logвҖҰ"` to record status mid-phase for later resume.
5. When the phase's acceptance criteria are met, `bd close <phase-id>`. The next phase auto-unblocks.

## Phase sequence

Phases run sequentially (each blocks the next). Within a phase subtasks are linear.

1. **Phase 1** вҖ” Enhanced Deny Hook (Component 1). New deny-enhanced mode, summary pipeline (ast-index subprocess + regex fallback + head-tail), wired into handleHookRead.
2. **Phase 2** вҖ” SessionStart Reminder (Component 2). New hook handler emitting the combined rules+agent-router message.
3. **Phase 3** вҖ” bless-agents CLI (Component 3). Scanner + classifier + writer + unbless + doctor drift check.
4. **Phase 4** вҖ” tp-* Subagent Templates (Component 4 content). Shared preamble, output-contract closing block, 6 Tier 1 agent .md files, golden fixture repo.
5. **Phase 5** вҖ” install-agents CLI (Component 4 distribution). Bundle templates in dist/agents, install/uninstall with user-chosen scope, MCP-startup stderr reminder, idempotence via frontmatter hash.
6. **Phase 6** вҖ” Telemetry & Config migration. hook-events.jsonl rotation, stats --by-agent command, config schema + migration (legacy deny вҶ’ advisory).
7. **Phase 7** вҖ” Integration, Benchmarks, Release. Composition E2E test (multiple PreToolUse hooks), benchmark suite, README + CHANGELOG, version bump, publish.

## TDD discipline

Every subtask follows the standard cycle (see superpowers:test-driven-development):

1. Write failing test.
2. Run test, verify failure message is the one you expect.
3. Write minimal code to pass.
4. Run test, verify pass.
5. Refactor if needed (tests still green).
6. Commit with meaningful message.

## Scope boundary for v0.20.0

- Tier 1 agents only (6 of them). Tier 2 deferred to v0.20.x.
- No marketplace submission (deferred to v0.21 per TP-816 --notes).
- No machine-parseable JSON output mode for agents (deferred to v0.21).

## Non-negotiables

- Hook failure paths always pass-through вҖ” never abort the agent (В§13 of TP-816).
- No silent writes to user's home dir (В§5.4.5).
- Every 'tp-*' agent's response respects its declared budget in В§5.4.9 (CI-enforced).
- Path safety: realpath + startsWith(projectRoot) check on every file read by the hook.

## Handoff

On completion of Phase 7 with all acceptance criteria green: update TP-816 status to 'closed', bump package.json to 0.20.0, npm publish, tag git. v0.21 work begins from the TP-816 --notes checklist.

title: Design: token-pilot enforcement layer (v0.20 вҶ’ v0.21)
description: Four-component architecture (enhanced deny + SessionStart reminder + bless-agents CLI + tp-* subagent family) to enforce token-pilot usage across main agent and all subagent categories without breaking public users. Replaces rejected transparent-substitution design. Target: v0.20.0 ships Tier 1 agents opt-in, v0.21.0 default.
design:
# Design: token-pilot Enforcement Layer

**Status:** Proposed
**Date:** 2026-04-17
**Target versions:** v0.20.0 (opt-in) вҶ’ v0.21.0 (default)
**Authors:** token-pilot team
**Reviewers:** system-architect-reviewer (2026-04-17)

---

## 1. TL;DR

token-pilot currently relies on an advisory PreToolUse hook and an MCP server. Observations show that (a) agents ignore advisory hints in long sessions, (b) rules are forgotten after `/clear` or `/compact`, and (c) specialised subagents without MCP access cannot use token-pilot tools at all. A previously considered "transparent Read substitution" design was found to have show-stopper flaws (Edit corruption, cache-as-file-path poisoning, dangerous line-number drift).

This document proposes a four-component enforcement layer that fixes all three observations without path substitution and without breaking any existing user:

1. **Enhanced deny mode** вҖ” hook returns a structural summary inside `permissionDecisionReason`. Works for every agent, including those with no MCP access.
2. **SessionStart reminder hook** вҖ” re-injects token-pilot rules and available-agent hints after every new session / `/clear` / `/compact`.
3. **`token-pilot bless-agents` CLI** вҖ” interactive tool that creates project-level overrides for restricted subagents so they can opt into token-pilot MCP.
4. **Subagent family (`tp-*`)** вҖ” a set of token-pilot-native subagents (вүҲ11 total, split Tier 1 in v0.20.0 and Tier 2 in v0.20.x) with tightly scoped system prompts that guarantee MCP-first behaviour. Main agent discovers them via standard Claude Code agent registry; reminder in Component 2 lists the right agent per task.

---

## 2. Problem Statement

### 2.1 Observed Pain

| Pain | Symptom | Impact |
|------|---------|--------|
| P1 | Agent sees advisory hint, proceeds with Read anyway. | 40вҖ“60 % potential savings lost in long sessions. |
| P2 | After `/clear` or `/compact`, agent acts as if token-pilot doesn't exist. | Rules in `CLAUDE.md` don't propagate into model's active memory. |
| P3 | Specialised subagents (`acc-*`, many built-ins) have restrictive `tools:` allowlists without `mcp__token-pilot__*`. | Subagents **physically** cannot call token-pilot tools even if instructed. |
| P4 | A previous idea (hard-block all `Read` on large code files) would break users whose subagents have no MCP fallback. | Unacceptable for a public npm package. |

### 2.2 Non-goals

- Forcing every agent everywhere to adopt token-pilot tool names.
- Rewriting Claude Code's agent-allowlist model.
- Providing token savings for non-code files (configs, docs, binaries).

---

## 3. Current State

- PreToolUse hook on `Read`, `Edit` registered via plugin `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json`.
- `handleHookRead` (src/index.ts:165) denies Read on code files > 300 lines with an advisory message.
- MCP server exposes ~20 tools (`smart_read`, `read_symbol`, `read_for_edit`, `outline`, `explore_area`, `find_usages`, etc.).
- Plugin defines SKILL files (`skills/guide`, `skills/install`, `skills/stats`).
- No SessionStart hook.
- No per-agent telemetry.
- No tooling that interacts with third-party agent definitions.

---

## 4. Rejected Alternative: Transparent Read Substitution

**The idea (rejected).** Hook redirects `Read(code.ts)` to a cached summary via `updatedInput.file_path`, so the agent "thinks" it read the original.

**Why it fails (architectural review 2026-04-17):**

1. **Edit corruption.** Claude records the cache path as "the file"; subsequent `Edit(old_string, new_string)` uses strings from the summary, not the original вҖ” edits fail or target wrong regions.
2. **Mental-model poisoning.** Grep, Write, Bash can all reference the cache path; `Write(cache_path, ...)` silently corrupts the cache.
3. **Line-number drift.** `L47` in summary вү  line 47 in the original; `read_range` and shell commands break.

Full rebuttal would require hooking Edit, Write, Grep, Bash, Read with a shared redirection table вҖ” unviable for a public package.

**Carried forward:** honest `file_path`, `updatedInput` reserved for sanitisation, explicit signal to the agent when it received a summary. See `docs/superpowers/reviews/...` archive for the full review.

---

## 5. Solution: Four-Component Enforcement Layer

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
```

### 5.1 Component 1 вҖ” Enhanced Deny Mode

**What changes.** The existing `handleHookRead` keeps `permissionDecision: "deny"` on qualifying Reads but replaces the current advisory message with a **rich structural summary**.

**Flow.**

1. Agent calls `Read("src/big.ts")`.
2. Hook reads the file, classifies it (code ext? line count > threshold? not bounded?).
3. If it qualifies, hook runs the **Summary Generation Pipeline** (В§8) and builds a message like:
   ```
   File "src/big.ts" has 2000 lines (~5000 tokens). A structural summary
   follows; the full Read was denied to save context. For specific lines
   use Read with offset/limit. For edit context use
   mcp__token-pilot__read_for_edit(path, symbol). For the full file on
   purpose use Read with {"bypass": true}.

   === Imports ===
   L2  import ...
   L3  import ...

   === Exports / Public symbols ===
   L25 export function getVersion(): string
   L35 export async function main(cliArgs)
   L75 export async function startServer(cliArgs)
   ...
   ```
4. Hook returns this as `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecisionReason`.
5. Agent sees the Read denied **and already has the structural content** in context. It proceeds:
   - If it needs specific lines вҶ’ `Read(path, offset, limit)` (hook passes through).
   - If it needs edit context вҶ’ `read_for_edit` via MCP (Categories A, B) or bounded Read (Category C).
   - If it needs the full file вҶ’ `Read(path, {bypass: true})` *(see 5.1.3)*.

**Why this is safe.**

- `file_path` is never rewritten. Follow-up tool calls refer to the real file.
- The agent explicitly knows it received a summary, not content. No line-number confusion because the agent hasn't "read" anything it would reference back.
- No cache file sits in the agent's tool history. No `Write(cache_path, ...)` corruption risk.
- No Edit hook gymnastics. Edit works normally against the real file.
- Works for every agent **including Category C** (no MCP required) вҖ” the summary arrives as part of the deny message, which every tool caller can read.

**5.1.1 Differences from current deny.**

| Dimension | Current `deny` | Enhanced `deny` |
|-----------|----------------|-----------------|
| Reason payload | Advisory: "use smart_read" | Structural summary of the file |
| Agent gets actionable info | No (must retry with different tool) | Yes (summary is already in context) |
| Works for Category-C agents | No (no MCP fallback) | Yes |
| Triggers additional tool call | Often | Only when specific lines/edits needed |

**5.1.2 Summary composition contract.**

Each summary contains:
- Header: original path, line count, estimated token count, agent label.
- **Imports** block вҖ” one line per import (collapsed).
- **Exports / Public API** block вҖ” signature per line, with L-number referring to the original.
- **Key declarations** block вҖ” class/function/type declarations.
- **Footer**: how to get specifics (bounded Read, `read_for_edit`, `{bypass: true}`).

Line numbers in the summary are **explicitly labelled as original-file line numbers** and the agent is told to use them with `Read(path, offset=L, limit=N)`. This avoids the line-drift class of errors from the rejected substitution design (the agent never looked at a file it thinks is the original).

**5.1.3 Bypass mechanism.**

Three bypass paths, in order of preference for an agent to choose:

1. **Bounded Read** вҖ” `Read(path, offset, limit)` is always passed through. Primary way to get specific content.
2. **New MCP tool `read_full_file_expensive(path)`** вҖ” for agents that have MCP access and need the whole file. Named to discourage casual use; telemetry tracks calls.
3. **Env var `TOKEN_PILOT_BYPASS=1`** вҖ” process-wide escape for CI / debug sessions.

A fourth path вҖ” `Read(path, {bypass: true})` via a non-standard key in `tool_input` вҖ” was considered but dropped from the initial v0.20 scope until we empirically verify (a) that Claude Code's Read tool forwards unknown keys to the hook across different subagent types, and (b) that subagents with strict input schemas don't reject the call client-side. Test plan in В§17.6. If the test passes in v0.20.x, we add it as a fourth path in v0.21 without breaking changes.

**5.1.4 Why no separate reminder for subagents.**

Subagents run with their own prompt context; the SessionStart hook targets the top-level session (see В§18 Q7). For Category-A/B subagents with MCP access, the enhanced-deny message delivered on their first qualifying Read carries the full instruction set (summary + bypass options + MCP tool names). For Category-C subagents without MCP access, the same deny message still arrives and is equally actionable (bounded Read, env var). In both cases, the hook's own output is the reminder вҖ” no additional injection is needed. If telemetry in v0.20.x shows that subagents persistently fail to use bounded Read even after seeing the deny message, we will revisit with a per-first-call `additionalContext` reminder.

### 5.2 Component 2 вҖ” SessionStart Reminder Hook

**What changes.** New hook registered in `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json`:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "hooks": [
          {"type": "command", "command": "${NODE} ${SCRIPT} hook-session-start"}
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Behaviour.** On every session start, `/clear`, or `/compact`, the hook emits a single `additionalContext` block combining the mandatory-tool rules **and** agent-router guidance:

```
[token-pilot active]

MANDATORY вҖ” for code files, use these before raw Read:
  - mcp__token-pilot__smart_read(path)          вҖ” structural overview
  - mcp__token-pilot__read_symbol(path, sym)    вҖ” one function / class
  - mcp__token-pilot__read_for_edit(path, sym)  вҖ” exact text for editing
  - mcp__token-pilot__outline(path)             вҖ” symbol list
Raw Read is allowed only with offset/limit, or with TOKEN_PILOT_BYPASS=1.

WHEN DELEGATING вҖ” use the right token-pilot-native subagent:
  - tp-run              вҖ” general workhorse, MCP-first drop-in for general-purpose
  - tp-onboard          вҖ” exploring an unfamiliar repo
  - tp-pr-reviewer      вҖ” reviewing a diff or changeset
  - tp-impact-analyzer  вҖ” tracing what will break from a change
  - tp-refactor-planner вҖ” planning a refactor with precise context
  - tp-test-triage      вҖ” investigating test failures
  (Additional tp-* agents installed in this project: <auto-list>)

Budget summary: last session saved ~N tokens across M reads.
```

This reminder is **short by design** (~250 tokens including the agent list) вҖ” compact enough to not bloat the prompt, specific enough that the model remembers both the tool contract and the agent registry.

The `<auto-list>` placeholder is expanded at hook run-time by scanning `.claude-plugin/agents/` and `.claude/agents/` for `tp-*` names. Tier 2 agents installed later auto-appear without spec edits.

The reminder is only emitted if (a) `.token-pilot/` exists (token-pilot is active in this project) and (b) the mode is not `off`. Safe by default.

### 5.3 Component 3 вҖ” `token-pilot bless-agents` CLI

**What it does.** Helps users give Category-C agents full MCP access to token-pilot.

**Flow.**

```
$ token-pilot bless-agents
Scanning installed agents...
  вң“ general-purpose            tools: *               вҶ’ already has MCP access
  вң“ code-analyzer              tools: All tools       вҶ’ already has MCP access
  вң— acc-ddd-auditor            tools: Read, Bash,...  вҶ’ MISSING token-pilot MCP
  вң— acc-behavioral-auditor     tools: Read, Grep,...  вҶ’ MISSING token-pilot MCP
  вң— Explore                    tools: All tools except Write, Edit
                                       вҶ’ already has MCP access (exclusion)
  ...

Found 14 agents that would benefit from token-pilot MCP access.
Create project-level overrides with MCP tools added?

  [a] Yes, all
  [i] Interactive (ask per agent)
  [n] No, cancel

> a

Creating overrides in .claude/agents/...
  вң“ .claude/agents/acc-ddd-auditor.md (added mcp__token-pilot__*)
  вң“ .claude/agents/acc-behavioral-auditor.md (added mcp__token-pilot__*)
  ...

Done. 14 agents now have access to token-pilot MCP.
Original definitions remain intact вҖ” run `token-pilot unbless-agents` to remove overrides.
```

**How it works.**

1. Scan discovery locations in priority order: project `.claude/agents/`, user `~/.claude/agents/`, plugin-contributed agents.
2. For each agent, parse frontmatter `tools:` field.
3. Classify:
   - **A** вҖ” `tools: *` or `All tools` вҶ’ already has MCP access вҶ’ skip.
   - **B** вҖ” `tools: All tools except ...` and `mcp__token-pilot__*` not excluded вҶ’ has access вҶ’ skip.
   - **C** вҖ” explicit list without `mcp__token-pilot__*` вҶ’ candidate for bless.
4. For each Category-C agent:
   - If project override already exists вҶ’ skip (respect user choice).
   - Otherwise, write a new project-level `.md` that *references* the upstream agent and adds MCP tools.

**Override file format.**

```markdown
---
name: acc-ddd-auditor
description: (inherited from upstream)
tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate, mcp__token-pilot__smart_read, mcp__token-pilot__read_symbol, mcp__token-pilot__read_for_edit, mcp__token-pilot__outline, mcp__token-pilot__find_usages
token_pilot:
  blessed: true
  upstream: plugin
  blessed_at: "2026-04-17T20:00:00Z"
  token_pilot_version: "0.20.0"
---

# acc-ddd-auditor (token-pilot blessed)

This agent's tool allowlist was extended with token-pilot MCP tools by
`token-pilot bless-agents`. The upstream system prompt and behaviour are
preserved by delegation.

{{ upstream_system_prompt }}
```

The `{{ upstream_system_prompt }}` template placeholder is resolved at bless time by copying the upstream file's body. If the upstream changes, users are warned by `token-pilot doctor` (В§16.4).

**Safety properties.**

- **Opt-in.** Nothing runs without user invoking `bless-agents`.
- **Reversible.** `unbless-agents` removes all blessed files.
- **Non-destructive.** Only creates new project-level overrides; never edits upstream.
- **Discoverable drift.** `doctor` checks upstream agent hashes; warns if upstream changed after blessing.
- **Respects user's prior overrides.** If `.claude/agents/<name>.md` already exists without the `blessed` marker, skip it.

### 5.4 Component 4 вҖ” Subagent Family (`tp-*`)

**Intent.** Ship token-pilot-native subagents whose tool allowlists and system prompts we fully control. Users that invoke them get a hard guarantee of MCP-first behaviour вҖ” no advisory, no blessing, no drift risk. This complements (does not replace) the other three components: the hook is still the universal safety net; the SessionStart reminder makes the family discoverable; `bless-agents` upgrades third-party agents users already rely on.

**Why this is the right shape.** Most token-pilot tools are useless in isolation and valuable in workflows (`project_overview` вҶ’ `explore_area` вҶ’ `read_symbol`; or `smart_diff` + `outline` + `find_usages` for review). An agent encapsulates one workflow, picks its own 3вҖ“5 tools, and gives the main agent a single named entrypoint for the whole job. This covers all 20 MCP tools through the agent layer, not just the 2вҖ“3 the hook typically triggers.

#### 5.4.1 Tier 1 вҖ” v0.20.0 (6 agents, MUST ship)

| Agent | User intent ("I want toвҖҰ") | Core tools |
|-------|---------------------------|------------|
| `tp-run` | "do something general, save tokens" | any (MCP-first) |
| `tp-onboard` | "I'm new to this repo, orient me" | `project_overview`, `explore_area`, `related_files`, `outline` |
| `tp-pr-reviewer` | "review this diff / changeset" | `smart_diff`, `outline`, `find_usages`, `read_symbol` |
| `tp-impact-analyzer` | "what will break if I change X?" | `find_usages`, `module_info`, `related_files` |
| `tp-refactor-planner` | "plan a refactor with exact context" | `read_for_edit`, `find_usages`, `read_diff`, `outline` |
| `tp-test-triage` | "understand why tests are failing" | `test_summary`, `smart_read`, `read_range`, `find_usages` |

#### 5.4.2 Tier 2 вҖ” v0.20.x (5 agents, NICE-TO-HAVE, ship as telemetry justifies)

| Agent | User intent | Core tools |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| `tp-dead-code-hunter` | "find dead code" | `find_unused`, `find_usages` |
| `tp-api-surveyor` | "what does this module expose as public API?" | `module_info`, `outline`, `find_usages` |
| `tp-history-explorer` | "what happened in this area recently?" | `smart_log`, `smart_diff`, `outline` |
| `tp-doc-navigator` | "structurally read docs / configs" | non-code section handlers (md / yaml / json / csv) |
| `tp-audit-scanner` | "find TODOs, FIXMEs, smells" | `code_audit`, `find_usages` |
| `tp-session-restorer` | "restore prior session context" | `session_snapshot` |

#### 5.4.3 Naming convention

- **Task-focused, not tool-focused.** Name encodes the intent the user brings, not the MCP tool used under the hood. `tp-impact-analyzer` > `tp-find-usages-agent`.
- **Verb or role.** Every name is a recognisable role (`run`, `reviewer`, `planner`, `hunter`, `surveyor`, `navigator`, `triage`) or an -er/-or noun. This is how the main agent recognises "this subagent can do this kind of work".
- **`tp-` prefix is load-bearing.** It groups the family in the agent registry, tells the main agent "MCP-first guarantee here", and is the hook of the family brand. Non-negotiable.

#### 5.4.4 System prompt architecture

Every `tp-*` agent shares a **base preamble** and adds an **agent-specific role block**. This keeps prompts short and consistent; a change to the token-pilot contract touches one file.

**Base preamble (shared, ~12 lines).**
```
You are a token-pilot agent (`tp-<name>`). Your defining contract:

For every file in a programming language, you MUST use the token-pilot MCP tools
(`mcp__token-pilot__smart_read`, `read_symbol`, `read_for_edit`, `outline`,
`find_usages`, `explore_area`, `project_overview`) before considering raw Read.
Raw Read is allowed only with explicit offset/limit, or when MCP tools have
already been tried and do not fit the task вҖ” in which case you must say so
in your reasoning. Never dump a file's full contents unless absolutely
necessary.

If any MCP tool fails, fall back sensibly (another MCP tool вҶ’ bounded Read вҶ’
pass-through) and note the fallback in your output. Never silently abandon
the contract.

Your specific role is defined below.
```

**Agent-specific role block (~10вҖ“30 lines per agent).** Describes the workflow this agent encodes, which tools it primarily reaches for, and when it should hand back to the caller. Example for `tp-impact-analyzer`:

```
Role: impact analysis. When given a symbol, file, or change description,
you must:
  1. Locate the change surface via `read_symbol` or `outline`.
  2. Enumerate downstream dependents via `find_usages` (direct + transitive
     to one hop).
  3. Inspect each dependent with `read_symbol` or bounded Read to judge
     compatibility.
  4. Report: list of affected sites, compatibility verdict per site, and a
     blast-radius summary.

Do not propose fixes. Your job is the honest impact map.
```

#### 5.4.5 Packaging & distribution

**Key constraint.** token-pilot is distributed primarily as an npm package, not as a Claude Code plugin via marketplace. Empirically verified (2026-04-17): `.claude-plugin/agents/*.md` is **only** auto-loaded when the plugin has been installed through Claude Code's plugin system (entry in `~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json`). A typical `npm install token-pilot` user does not get this path; their Claude Code scans `~/.claude/agents/` and the project's `.claude/agents/` for agent registry.

**Distribution model for v0.20.**

Agent markdown files are bundled in the npm package at `dist/agents/tp-*.md` (built from source `templates/agents/tp-*.md`). They are NOT placed in `.claude-plugin/agents/` because that would only work for marketplace-installed plugins. Instead, a CLI command copies them into one of the two standard locations Claude Code actually scans.

**The CLI is the canonical path; scope is always the user's explicit choice, mirroring Claude Code's own `/plugin install` UX.**

```bash
# Interactive (TTY) вҖ” asks the user where to install
npx token-pilot install-agents

  Where should token-pilot agents be installed?
    [1] user     вҶ’ ~/.claude/agents/tp-*.md (available in every project)
    [2] project  вҶ’ .claude/agents/tp-*.md (only this project)
  Choice [1/2]: _

# Non-interactive вҖ” script / CI form
npx token-pilot install-agents --scope=user
npx token-pilot install-agents --scope=project

# Removal
npx token-pilot uninstall-agents
npx token-pilot uninstall-agents --scope=user
```

**No silent auto-install.** At MCP server startup, if no `tp-*` agents are detected in either scope, the server emits exactly ONE stderr line:

```
[token-pilot] tp-* agents not installed. Run `npx token-pilot install-agents` to enable guaranteed-savings subagents (scope is your choice: user or project).
```

The reminder can be silenced with `TOKEN_PILOT_NO_AGENT_REMINDER=1` or `agents.reminder: false` in config. It never auto-installs, never prompts inside the MCP server (MCP has no TTY), and never writes files the user has not explicitly requested.

**Idempotence and versioning.**

- Each installed agent file has `token_pilot_version: "<semver>"` and `token_pilot_content_hash: "<sha256-of-template>"` in its frontmatter.
- Re-running `install-agents` updates agent files whose `content_hash` matches the prior version's template (i.e., the user did not edit them). Hand-edited files are preserved and the CLI emits `"вҡ  tp-<name>.md edited by user, skipping update."`.
- A `--force` flag on `install-agents` overrides and re-installs from templates, with a confirmation prompt.

**Parallel path вҖ” Claude Code marketplace submission (Vision for v0.21+).**

Independently of the CLI path, we submit token-pilot as a proper Claude Code plugin to one of the public marketplaces (`anthropics/claude-plugins-official` or our own). Users then have two installation options:

1. **Via npm** (current, CLI-driven agent install): `npm install token-pilot` + `npx token-pilot install-agents`.
2. **Via Claude Code**: `/plugin install token-pilot`. The plugin bundle includes `.claude-plugin/agents/tp-*.md`, `.claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json`, and the MCP manifest. Auto-loads everything natively.

Both paths converge on the same agent definitions вҖ” the marketplace package simply ships the same files in the plugin-native location. No fork, no divergence.

The marketplace path is intended for v0.21+ вҖ” it requires coordination with Claude Code release cycles and is not a v0.20 blocker. v0.20 ships solely through npm.

#### 5.4.6 Adoption mechanism (no new component)

We explicitly do **not** add a separate "agent router" component. The family is discovered via three existing channels:

1. **SessionStart reminder (В§5.2).** Lists the installed `tp-*` agents once per session with one-line descriptions.
2. **Agent frontmatter `description:` field.** Claude Code shows this when the main agent is choosing a delegate. Descriptions are written task-first (see В§5.4.3).
3. **README / CHANGELOG.** The public-facing documentation path.

No CLAUDE.md auto-mutation. No invisible routing. Main agent chooses based on the normal Claude Code delegation flow; our contribution is making the right choice obvious.

#### 5.4.7 Tier gating

- Tier 1 ships in v0.20.0. Six agents are enough to cover the high-frequency workflows (general work, onboarding, review, impact, refactor, tests).
- Tier 2 ships agent-by-agent in v0.20.x, gated by telemetry: we ship the next agent when the SessionStart reminder's corresponding task-intent log shows вүҘ 5 uses per active user per week without a matching `tp-*` being invoked. This prevents prompt bloat from agents users don't reach for.
- Maintenance ceiling: 15 agents. Beyond that, we reject additions until we decommission one. Keeps the family navigable in the reminder and the main agent's delegate list.

#### 5.4.8 Testing strategy (agent-specific, complements В§17)

- **Per-agent smoke test.** A scripted task (fixture repo + prompt) runs under the agent and asserts: (a) MCP tool called at least once before any raw Read, (b) raw-Read count вүӨ threshold, (c) output contains the expected artefact shape.
- **Golden comparison.** Same task run under `tp-run` vs `general-purpose` вҖ” assert `tp-run` uses вүӨ 40 % of the tokens used by `general-purpose` on the fixture. Regression if this slips.
- **Prompt contract test.** Parse each agent's frontmatter + body, verify the shared preamble is present, tools list contains `mcp__token-pilot__*`, description is non-empty and non-generic.
- **Behaviour drift alert.** If an agent's token usage on the golden task increases > 20 % between versions, CI fails. Protects against accidental prompt edits that weaken the MCP-first discipline.
- **Output-budget test.** Each agent's response on the golden fixture is measured. Regression if response exceeds the В§5.4.9 budget by > 10 %. Protects the caller's context from accidental bloat.

#### 5.4.9 Output contract and context hygiene

A subagent that saves tokens on reads but returns a 3 000-token verbatim dump gives it all back. Output shape is as important as read efficiency.

**Adopt (from feature-dev agents):** numeric confidence gate with hard threshold; "pick one, commit" (no multi-option presentations); terminal "essential files" pointer list; `file:line` refs over pasted code.

**Forbid explicitly:** mandatory positive preamble; "comprehensive" without cap; "thorough but concise" (contradictory); inline code when `file:line` suffices; `<example>` blocks in `description:` frontmatter (they ship on every delegate-listing); tool-call narration ("I called smart_read, thenвҖҰ").

**Output contract enforced in every agent's preamble:**

1. Verdict on line 1 вҖ” the caller can stop there.
2. Named bold sections; one bullet = one finding.
3. Filter to a declared bar (confidence вүҘ N, severity вүҘ X) вҖ” below the bar вҶ’ not shown.
4. `file:line` refs only; no pasted source unless the role requires a patch.
5. No tool narration, no operational log.
6. Overflow вҶ’ artefact: findings over budget write to `.token-pilot/<agent>-<ts>.md`; visible response stays within budget.

**Per-agent response budgets (tokens, tightenable in v0.20.x):**

| Agent | Budget | Agent | Budget |
|-------|--------|-------|--------|
| `tp-run` | 800 | `tp-onboard` | 600 |
| `tp-pr-reviewer` | 600 | `tp-impact-analyzer` | 400 |
| `tp-refactor-planner` | 500 | `tp-test-triage` | 500 |
| `tp-dead-code-hunter` | 400 | `tp-api-surveyor` | 500 |
| `tp-history-explorer` | 500 | `tp-doc-navigator` | 400 |
| `tp-audit-scanner` | 500 | `tp-session-restorer` | 300 |

Enforced via В§5.4.8 output-budget test.

**Preamble closing block (appended to the shared preamble in В§5.4.4):**

```
RESPONSE CONTRACT:
- Lead with a one-line verdict.
- Use bold section headers; one finding per bullet.
- Reference code as `path:line`; paste source only if your role requires a patch.
- Do NOT narrate tool calls. Do NOT preamble with "what was done well".
- If findings exceed your budget, write overflow to
  `.token-pilot/<agent>-<timestamp>.md` and reference it; keep the
  visible response within budget.
```

**Machine-parseable output (v0.21+).** Each `tp-*` may emit a JSON block guarded by `--format=json` for agent chaining. Schema per agent post-marketplace. Not v0.20 scope.

## 8. Summary Generation Pipeline

The deny-enhanced message embeds a structural summary. Quality matters вҖ” poor summaries push the agent to re-read the file anyway, losing savings.

**Pipeline (fallback chain):** the hook is a short-lived Node process. It cannot share state with the long-running MCP server (MCP uses stdio to Claude Code), so parsers must run in-process or delegate to a separate binary.

1. **Primary вҖ” ast-index binary subprocess.** If `findBinary` locates the bundled ast-index, hook invokes `ast-index outline <file>` and parses the structured output. Gives exports, signatures, line refs. Typical ~20вҖ“60 ms warm.
2. **Fallback 1 вҖ” in-process regex parser.** Pattern-based scan for language-specific declarations (mirrors the prototype). Pure Node, no subprocess. Typical ~5вҖ“20 ms.
3. **Fallback 2 вҖ” truncated head + tail.** Last-resort structural proxy when parser crashes: first 40 lines + last 20 lines with a clear "parse failed, giving you head/tail" warning. Trivial cost.
4. **Fallback 3 вҖ” pass-through.** If even fallback 2 fails (disk error, encoding issue), emit `permissionDecision: "allow"` to let the original Read proceed. **Never return an error that blocks the agent.**

**Budget.** Summaries are capped at `summaryMaxTokens` (default 1200). If exceeded, the summary is trimmed and a note added: `(trimmed to fit budget; call outline(path) for full structure)`.

**Language support.** Primary parser supports TS/JS/Python/Go/Rust/Java/PHP/Ruby. For unsupported extensions, fallback 1 (regex) handles them coarsely.

## 9. Hook Implementation Details

### 9.1 Hook input contract

Expected stdin (from Claude Code):

```json
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "transcript_path": "...",
  "cwd": "...",
  "permission_mode": "default",
  "hook_event_name": "PreToolUse",
  "tool_name": "Read",
  "tool_input": { "file_path": "...", "offset": null, "limit": null },
  "agent_id": "...",     // optional, present for subagents
  "agent_type": "..."    // optional, present for subagents
}
```

### 9.2 Hook output contract (enhanced deny)

```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "deny",
    "permissionDecisionReason": "<summary text>"
  }
}
```

For pass-through: `exit 0` with empty stdout.
For bypass (flag or bounded): `exit 0` with empty stdout.
For failure: `exit 0` with empty stdout (degrade to pass-through; log to stderr).

### 9.3 Hook process lifecycle

**Latency target.** p50 < 30 ms, p95 < 100 ms, p99 < 250 ms.

**Strategy.**

- Hook script is short-lived Node process; cold start ~80вҖ“150 ms (Node init + script parse).
- Primary parser invokes `ast-index` binary as subprocess (~20вҖ“60 ms warm via OS fork/exec).
- Fallback to in-process regex parser (pure Node, ~5вҖ“20 ms) if `ast-index` is unavailable.
- Fallback to head+tail view (~5 ms) if regex parser errors.

**Future optimisation (out of scope for v0.20).** Long-running hook daemon with persistent ast-index state, reducing warm latency to ~5вҖ“10 ms. Deferred until benchmarks show the sub-process strategy is a real UX problem. Design leaves room for this: swap the parser invocation layer without changing the hook contract.

## 10. Security

### 10.1 Path safety

- **Problem:** `resolve(cwd, path)` does not prevent `../../../etc/passwd`.
- **Mitigation:** `realpath` the resolved path; require it to `startsWith(projectRoot + path.sep)`; deny-with-error-pass-through otherwise.

### 10.2 Summary content leakage

- **Concern:** Summary reveals structural info about private code.
- **Mitigation:** Summary is emitted only back to the same session that requested the Read. It is never written to disk in enhanced-deny mode (cache removed from design). Stderr logging is opt-in via `TOKEN_PILOT_DEBUG`.

### 10.3 Hook input trust

- Hook input comes from Claude Code. Treated as untrusted вҖ” validate all fields (no `JSON.parse` of unchecked size, string-length caps on paths, refuse `file_path` containing null bytes).

### 10.4 CI / sandboxed environments

- If the hook cannot resolve `projectRoot` (no git, read-only FS, sandbox), it emits pass-through and logs a single stderr note. Must **never** abort the agent's workflow.

## 11. Performance & Latency

| Operation | Target | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Hook cold start (Node) | < 150 ms | Unavoidable today; mitigated because hook only fires on large code Reads. |
| Summary via MCP (warm) | < 30 ms | IPC + ast-index lookup. |
| Summary via regex | < 30 ms | Pure Node, no IPC. |
| Summary via head+tail | < 5 ms | Trivial. |
| End-to-end hook (warm) | < 100 ms p95 | Combines cold start + summary. |
| End-to-end hook (cold) | < 250 ms p99 | Includes Node cold start in adverse environments. |

**Overhead budget.** For a session with 30 qualifying Reads, worst-case hook overhead is ~7.5 s cold or ~3 s warm. In exchange for ~70 % token reduction, this is a justified trade. We will ship benchmarks in `docs/performance/2026-04-17-hook-benchmark.md` to validate.

**Mitigation levers available later.** Persistent hook daemon, bytecode cache, skipping the MCP IPC for small files.

## 12. Hook Chain Composition

**Problem.** Users may have multiple plugins registering `PreToolUse:Read` hooks (e.g. `context-mode` seen in the wild).

**Claude Code behaviour (confirmed from official docs, 2026-04-17 verification).**

Claude Code defines an explicit precedence for resolving conflicting PreToolUse decisions:

> *"When multiple PreToolUse hooks return conflicting decisions, the system follows a specific precedence order: **deny, defer, ask, and finally allow**. This ensures that restrictive decisions take priority over permissive ones."*
>
> *"A PreToolUse hook returning `deny` will cancel the tool call regardless of other hook outcomes."*
>
> *"Text from `additionalContext` is accumulated from all hooks and passed to Claude."*

This resolves the ambiguity we previously worried about. Our design benefits directly:

1. **Enhanced deny wins over any competing `allow`.** If another plugin's hook says "allow" and ours says "deny", our deny is final. Our users get our summary regardless of what else is installed.
2. **SessionStart reminder is additive, not exclusive.** Our reminder text is accumulated with any other plugin's `additionalContext`, not replaced. We never fight another plugin for the reminder slot.
3. **Our design never uses `updatedInput`** (see В§4 for why), so the one remaining undefined-behaviour area вҖ” `updatedInput` chaining across hooks вҖ” is not on our critical path.

**Remaining edge cases.**

- **Two plugins both `deny`.** Both reasons accumulate via `additionalContext`? Or only the first wins? Docs don't specify. Low-impact since either outcome serves the user (they get a denial and at least one useful reason). Will empirically verify in v0.20.x integration tests.
- **A plugin returns `ask` while we return `deny`.** By precedence, deny wins, user is not prompted. Intended behaviour вҖ” we don't want other plugins pulling users into permission dialogues we meant to silently handle.

**Escalation.** If a real composition issue surfaces, we add a `hookPriority` config key and document install-order best practices. Not a v0.20.0 blocker вҖ” the platform precedence rules are sufficient for our design's correctness.

**Testing requirement.** В§17 adds one integration test: install token-pilot alongside a mock plugin that returns `allow`, run a deny-qualifying Read, assert our deny wins and our reason reaches the model.

## 13. Failure Modes

Every failure path must degrade to **pass-through, never abort**.

| Failure | Handling |
|---------|----------|
| Stdin malformed JSON | exit 0, no output (pass-through). Stderr log. |
| File missing / unreadable | exit 0, no output. |
| Summary pipeline all fallbacks fail | exit 0, no output. Stderr log. |
| ast-index subprocess fails | Fall through to regex parser; no user-visible error. |
| `projectRoot` resolution fails | exit 0, no output. Stderr log once per session. |
| Node crash mid-execution | Hook exits non-zero; Claude Code treats as advisory (documented fallback). |

A **contract test** asserts each of these paths. Regressions here are release-blocking.

### 14.1 Version roadmap

| Version | Default mode | New features | User action |
|---------|--------------|--------------|-------------|
| v0.19.x | `deny` (old) | вҖ” | None. |
| **v0.20.0** | `advisory` (auto-migrated from old `deny`) | `deny-enhanced` mode opt-in. `SessionStart` hook opt-in. `bless-agents` CLI. **Tier 1 `tp-*` subagents** (tp-run, tp-onboard, tp-pr-reviewer, tp-impact-analyzer, tp-refactor-planner, tp-test-triage) shipped via `install-agents` CLI. | Auto-migration of `mode: "deny"` вҶ’ `"advisory"` with stderr notice. Users run `npx token-pilot install-agents` once to enable the agent family (prompts for user/project scope). |
| v0.20.x | unchanged | Bugfixes, benchmarks, docs. **Tier 2 `tp-*` subagents** ship agent-by-agent as telemetry justifies (see В§5.4.7). On upgrade, `install-agents` re-run updates unchanged files automatically. | Re-run `npx token-pilot install-agents` to pull new Tier 2 agents (idempotent for unchanged files). |
| **v0.21.0** | `deny-enhanced` | New default. `SessionStart` hook auto-registered. Optional: marketplace plugin submission so `/plugin install token-pilot` works as an alternative distribution path. | Users preferring minimal intervention set `mode: "advisory"`. |

**Old `deny` removal note.** Because the old `deny` mode is removed entirely, a one-time auto-migration runs on v0.20 startup: configs with `mode: "deny"` are rewritten to `mode: "advisory"` with a stderr notice explaining the change. Alternative would be to rewrite to `deny-enhanced` automatically. We choose `advisory` (the more conservative choice) so users do not unexpectedly see new verbose output until they opt in.

**Agent family rollout note.** Tier 1 `tp-*` agents are bundled inside the npm package under `dist/agents/` but are **not** auto-written to the user's filesystem. A single explicit CLI command вҖ” `npx token-pilot install-agents` вҖ” copies them to the user-chosen scope (`~/.claude/agents/` or `./.claude/agents/`). Scope is always the user's choice, mirroring Claude Code's `/plugin install` UX. At MCP startup, if `tp-*` agents are absent, the server logs one stderr reminder to run the command (silenceable via config or env var). No silent writes to the user's home directory.

Tier 2 agents ship agent-by-agent in v0.20.x. On each npm upgrade that introduces new Tier 2 agents, the stderr reminder resumes until the user re-runs `install-agents` to pull them in. Idempotence protects any hand-edited agent files.

### 14.2 Communication

- CHANGELOG entry with "what changed, how to keep the old behaviour, why we changed it", including a section on the new `tp-*` family and a one-line install command.
- One-line message on first v0.20 start: *"token-pilot v0.20 available. Try `npx token-pilot install-agents` for guaranteed-savings subagents, or `token-pilot config set mode=deny-enhanced` for the enhanced hook."*
- README section "Quick start" updated with the single `install-agents` command, the scope prompt, and a note about session restart.
- v0.21 release notes pinned to README and MCP manifest.

### 14.3 Rollback path

Every new feature is keyed to a config flag or reversible CLI action.

- `mode: "advisory"` restores pre-v0.20 behaviour (minus the old `deny` mode which is removed вҖ” `advisory` is the closest preserved equivalent).
- `SessionStart` hook вҶ’ disabled via `sessionStart.enabled = false`.
- `bless-agents` вҶ’ undone by `unbless-agents`.
- `install-agents` вҶ’ undone by `uninstall-agents` (removes only token-pilot's own `tp-*` files; never touches user agents outside the family).
- Stderr reminder for `install-agents` вҶ’ silenced via `agents.reminder = false` or `TOKEN_PILOT_NO_AGENT_REMINDER=1`.

## 16. `bless-agents` CLI вҖ” Detailed Spec

### 16.1 Commands

```
token-pilot bless-agents [--auto|--interactive|--dry-run]
token-pilot unbless-agents [--all|<name>...]
token-pilot agents list
token-pilot agents diff <name>    # show upstream vs blessed
token-pilot doctor                # includes agent-drift check
```

### 16.2 Scan locations (precedence: highest first)

1. `./.claude/agents/**/*.md`
2. `~/.claude/agents/**/*.md`
3. `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/agents/**/*.md` (plugin-contributed)
4. Built-in agents are **not** in the filesystem; they cannot be blessed вҖ” documented limitation.

### 16.3 Built-in agents

Claude Code built-ins (e.g. `general-purpose`, `Plan`) are defined by Claude Code itself, not by files we can read. Two paths:

- **Most built-ins are Category A/B** (have `*` or `All tools except ...`) so they already have MCP access.
- For any built-in in Category C, users must create a manual project-level override. `bless-agents` documents this in its summary output.

### 16.4 Drift detection

- On bless, store `upstream_hash = sha256(upstream file content)` in the blessed override's frontmatter.
- `token-pilot doctor` walks all blessed agents and recomputes upstream hashes. Warns on mismatch with remediation: `token-pilot re-bless <name>`.

### 16.5 Safety contract

- Never modifies upstream agent files.
- Never touches a project agent that lacks the `blessed: true` marker.
- `--dry-run` shows the diff without writing.
- Writes are atomic (temp file + rename).

### 16.6 Discovery path for existing users (not first-install)

Most v0.19 users already have token-pilot installed and will never run `init` again. For them, three discovery paths:

1. **Version-change nudge.** `.token-pilot-fingerprint.json` already tracks the installed version. On first v0.20 run after upgrade, emit a one-time stderr notice: *"token-pilot v0.20 adds `bless-agents` вҖ” run `npx token-pilot bless-agents` to extend savings to specialised subagents."* The notice is keyed to a `noticedVersions: ["0.20.0"]` entry in the fingerprint so it only fires once.
2. **SessionStart nudge (opt-in).** When `sessionStart.showStats: true`, the session-start reminder appends *"(14 subagents without MCP access detected вҖ” run `token-pilot bless-agents` to fix)"* exactly once per session when there are unblessed Category-C agents present. Never nags.
3. **CHANGELOG + README.** Standard release-notes path. Pinned entry at the top of the README.

All three paths are passive вҖ” the user always drives the actual `bless-agents` invocation.

### 16.7 First-install discovery

For fresh installs (new user running `token-pilot init` for the first time in v0.20+):

```
$ token-pilot init
Installing token-pilot...
вң“ Hook registered
вң“ MCP config added

Scanning installed agents...
  Found 14 subagents without MCP access.
  These agents could use token-pilot via a one-command upgrade.
  Run bless-agents now? [y/N]: _
```

- `y` вҶ’ runs `bless-agents --auto`.
- `N` вҶ’ continues without blessing; user can always run `bless-agents` later.

Interactive prompt is skipped when stdin is not a TTY (CI / automation).

### 16.8 Claude Code session scope вҖ” newly-written agents require a session restart

**Empirically verified (2026-04-17).** Claude Code loads the plugin agent registry at session start. Adding a new `.claude-plugin/agents/*.md` or a new project-level override in `.claude/agents/*.md` during a running session does **not** make the agent available to that session. Invoking the fresh name returns `Agent type '<name>' not found`.

**Implications for every command that writes agent files:**

- `token-pilot bless-agents` вҖ” after writing overrides, tell the user: *"Blessed N agents. **Start a new Claude Code session** to pick them up."* Include this line in CLI output and `doctor`.
- `token-pilot init` (when it proactively offers bless) вҖ” include the same notice.
- `token-pilot unbless-agents` вҖ” same notice; removing files from a live session won't hide them either.
- Tier 1 / Tier 2 `tp-*` rollout вҖ” on every npm upgrade of `token-pilot`, log a stderr line once: *"token-pilot updated: new agents available. Start a new Claude Code session to see tp-\<name\>."*

**Desktop app guidance** added to the user-facing docs:

- **macOS / Windows app**: open a new conversation (the app's "New Chat" / `вҢҳN` equivalent) in the same project, or fully quit (`вҢҳQ`) and relaunch if new chat still does not show the agents.
- **CLI `claude`**: exit the current session and start `claude` again.

**Testing.** `token-pilot doctor --restart-hint` command returns a human-readable block the user can paste if confused.

- Config loader precedence.
- `bless-agents` scanning and classification.

- `bless-agents` creating and undoing overrides against a fixture `.claude/agents/` tree.

### 17.3 Golden / snapshot tests

### 17.4 E2E

- Harness that boots Claude Code in dry mode with token-pilot installed, runs a scripted agent session, asserts hook fired and token counts match expectations.

- `npm run bench:hook` вҖ” measures p50 / p95 / p99 latency for the hook under cold and warm conditions. CI fails if p95 > 150 ms.

### 17.6 Empirical `tool_input` forwarding test

Must pass before we consider adding `Read(path, {bypass: true})` as a fourth bypass path:

**Test environment.** Claude Code with token-pilot installed. A debug hook that logs full `tool_input` to a file.

**Test matrix.** For each of: main agent, a Category-A subagent (`general-purpose`), a Category-B subagent (`Explore`), and a Category-C subagent (an `acc-*` that has `tools:` restricted but includes `Read`):

1. Agent calls `Read(path)` вҖ” baseline.
2. Agent calls `Read(path, {bypass: true})` вҖ” does the extra key arrive in the hook's stdin? Does the Read tool reject it client-side before the hook sees it?
3. Agent calls `Read(path, {frobnicate: 42})` вҖ” control for "any unknown key".

**Pass criteria.** All three subagent categories must forward unknown keys through to the hook for this bypass path to be viable. If any category rejects client-side, we cannot rely on the flag and stick with the three paths in В§5.1.3.

**Parking.** Test is not a v0.20.0 blocker. It runs in v0.20.x. If results are positive, we add the flag as a non-breaking change in v0.21. If negative, we document and drop the idea.

---

## 18. Open Questions

| # | Question | Default answer | Blocks ship? |
|---|----------|----------------|--------------|
| Q1 | Should the SessionStart reminder be a system reminder style block, or plain `additionalContext` text? | `additionalContext` text (safer, documented). | No |
| Q2 | Should the deny message include historical stats ("last session saved 72 %")? | Yes in v0.21, off by default in v0.20 to keep payload short. | No |
| Q3 | For Category-C built-ins we cannot bless, should we log a notice on first deny? | Log once per session, offer `bless-agents` command as remediation. | No |
| Q4 | Should `read_full_file_expensive` be gated behind a config flag? | Yes вҖ” off by default; users enable per project. | No |
| Q5 | For monorepos: one cache dir at the repo root, or one per package? | None in enhanced deny (no cache). Revisit if we add caching later. | No |
| Q6 | Does Claude Code's Read tool forward unknown keys in `tool_input` (like `{bypass: true}`) to the hook? | Parked вҖ” test in v0.20.x per В§17.6, accept only if all subagent categories pass. Meanwhile design has three bypass paths that do not depend on this. | No |
| Q7 | How does SessionStart hook interact with subagent spawns? Does it fire once per top-level session or for subagent sub-sessions too? | Expected: top-level only. Will confirm during implementation. Not a blocker вҖ” Category-C subagents learn via the deny-enhanced message, which arrives at their Read call regardless. | No |
| Q8 | Does the main agent actually pick `tp-*` agents when task-descriptions match, given our frontmatter `description:` fields, or does it default to built-ins? | Empirical test in v0.20.0-rc: run canonical fixtures (onboarding, PR review, impact, refactor, test triage) with both only-`tp-*` and only-built-ins available, compare selection rates. If main agent prefers built-ins, we tune `description:` copy. | No |
| Q9 | Are `tp-*` agents invoked with the same `additionalContext` that the main agent received, or do they start with an empty context window? | Standard Claude Code behaviour: subagents get their own fresh context. Our SessionStart hint doesn't help them. Mitigation is built in: the base preamble of every `tp-*` system prompt carries the contract, so subagent context is self-sufficient. | No |
| Q10 | Cold prompt length: shared preamble (~12 lines) + agent-specific block (~10вҖ“30 lines) + frontmatter. Is total вүӨ 60 lines per agent, so we do not bloat the main agent's delegate listing? | Target вүӨ 60 lines (description in frontmatter is what the main agent sees for routing; body is only loaded when agent is invoked). CI check on agent files. | No |
| Q11 | What format do bundled agent templates use inside the npm package вҖ” raw `.md` static files in `dist/agents/`, or inlined TypeScript string constants? | Static `.md` files at `dist/agents/tp-*.md`, copied from source `templates/agents/` at build time. Enables external inspection, simpler diffs on upgrades, no escaping pain. | No |
| Q12 | Handling of users who install both `user` and `project` scopes (e.g., global agents plus a project override): does `install-agents --scope=project` silently double the footprint? | Yes by design вҖ” Claude Code agent-precedence gives project > user, so a project file overrides. `doctor` surfaces this explicitly so the user knows. | No |

---

## 19. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| Agent re-reads after seeing summary ("I need to see the real file") | Medium | Undermines savings | Summary explicitly tells agent to use bounded Read for specifics; telemetry tracks re-read rate; threshold tuned in v0.20.x. |
| `additionalContext` token cost offsets savings | Low | Minor | SessionStart reminder capped at 250 tokens (incl. agent list); summary at 1200 tokens; both much smaller than typical large-file Read. |
| Claude Code API changes `hookSpecificOutput` shape | Low | High | Integration tests against Claude Code fixtures; CI verifies parsing; gated fallback emits plain stderr. |
| Hook composition issues with other plugins | Medium | Medium | E2E composition tests; documented install order; `hookPriority` escape hatch. |
| Users complain about "too wordy" deny messages | Medium | Low | `advisory` mode always available; `deny-enhanced` summary is capped. Users who preferred old `deny` silence are auto-migrated to `advisory`. |
| Agents reach for `read_full_file_expensive` casually | Low | Medium | Name designed to discourage; config-gated; telemetry tracks usage. |
| Path-traversal in hook | Low | High | realpath + startsWith check with unit tests. |
| Node cold-start latency perceptible to users | Medium | Low | Benchmark-gated; MCP IPC already warm; future daemon option. |
| Main agent does not delegate to `tp-*` agents even when appropriate | Medium | Medium | SessionStart reminder lists them; frontmatter `description:` is task-focused; Q8 empirical test before v0.20.0 final. |
| `tp-*` system prompt drift weakens the MCP-first discipline across releases | Low | High | Behaviour-drift CI test (В§5.4.8): golden task re-run, fail if token usage rises > 20 %. |
| Maintenance burden of 11 agents grows unsustainable | Medium | Medium | Shared base preamble keeps per-agent body small; hard ceiling of 15 agents (В§5.4.7); Tier 2 gated by real usage telemetry. |
| Main agent picks `tp-run` for trivial tasks where general-purpose would suffice, adding latency | Low | Low | `tp-run` description: "use when code-reads dominate the task"; benchmark justifies. |
| Community-authored agents collide with `tp-*` names | Low | Medium | We own the `tp-` prefix in documentation; if a project-level override of a `tp-*` exists without our blessed marker, `token-pilot doctor` warns. |
| User never runs `install-agents` вҶ’ never benefits from the `tp-*` family | Medium | Low | One-line stderr reminder on every MCP startup until installed; README quickstart leads with this command; telemetry tracks how many installs per npm-download. |
| User runs `install-agents --scope=user` on a shared machine, polluting other user accounts | Low | Low | `~/.claude/agents/` is per-user by OS convention; multi-user machines are rare for devtools. Documented. |
| Upgrade path bug: new npm version leaves stale `tp-*` files in user's agents dir | Medium | Medium | Hash-based idempotence (В§5.4.5): on `install-agents`, compare installed hash vs bundled template hash; update if unchanged, skip if user-edited, warn. Integration test per upgrade. |

---

## 20. Glossary

- **Advisory / Deny-enhanced:** hook modes. Advisory prints a hint; deny-enhanced blocks and returns the structural summary.
- **Category A/B/C subagent:** by MCP access via `tools:` allowlist вҖ” A/B have it (`*` / `All tools except вҖҰ`), C does not (explicit list without `mcp__token-pilot__*`).
- **Bless:** project-level override of a third-party subagent granting it MCP access.
- **Substitute (rejected):** would have swapped `file_path` to a cache path. See В§4.
- **Summary:** structural outline (imports, exports, symbols) from the summary pipeline.
- **Bypass:** escape paths from deny-enhanced вҖ” bounded Read, `read_full_file_expensive`, env `TOKEN_PILOT_BYPASS=1`.
- **`tp-*`:** token-pilot-native subagents we fully control (see В§5.4).
- **Tier 1 / Tier 2:** v0.20.0 must-ship vs v0.20.x telemetry-gated.
- **Shared preamble:** ~12-line MCP-first contract reused across every `tp-*` agent.

---

## Appendix A вҖ” Empirical findings

- Regex-based summary of `src/index.ts` (541 lines / ~4941 tokens) вҶ’ 103 lines / ~1427 tokens (72 % reduction). Validates summary-size assumptions in В§5.1 / В§11.
- `updatedInput` substitution is technically supported by Claude Code. Rejected for Edit/mental-model reasons (В§4).
- Empirical: plugin agents in `.claude-plugin/agents/` are not auto-loaded unless token-pilot is installed as a marketplace plugin. Drove В§5.4.5 CLI-based distribution.
- Empirical: adding a new agent file mid-session does not hot-reload вҖ” requires a new Claude Code session (В§16.8).

## Appendix B вҖ” Review Record

External architectural review by `system-architect-reviewer` (2026-04-17): 3 show-stoppers + 7 serious concerns in the initial substitution design. All incorporated throughout (В§4, В§В§10вҖ“13).
